steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch one long, meandering line for the main vine — let it bend and curl rather than running straight. This sets the energy of the whole plant.

steps.title: Branch out (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, branch two or three shorter vines off the main line, each curving in its own direction. Vary the angles — no two branches should leave at the same tilt.

steps.title: Mark the blossom placements (pencil)
steps.description: Along the vines, lightly sketch four or five small circles where the flowers will sit. Cluster them unevenly — a couple together, one off on its own — rather than spacing them like beads.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the vines
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace the main vine and its branches in confident strokes, letting the line wobble where the pencil wobbled. The little imperfections give the plant its life.

steps.title: Ink the roses (ink)
steps.description: At each circle, draw a rose as a small swirl of overlapping curves — start from a tiny center loop and spiral outward two or three times. Each rose only needs three or four strokes.

steps.title: Add leaves (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, scatter pointed-oval leaves along the vines — two strokes meeting at each tip. Don't draw every leaf; a handful at varied angles is enough to suggest fullness.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the inked rose. A faint construction line through a leaf is fine — leave any that read as nice texture.
